notes: Main computing platform at PHI service bureau; CPU time cost $360/hour in 1969.
Family platform for PHI service bureau operations; reference card was primary documentation resource.
Primary language used at PHI; Siegel's One-Instruction Switch optimization was demonstrable in COBOL; Cosmos generated millions of lines of architecturally sound COBOL.

Code-generation system at PHI that produced millions of lines of COBOL per specification. Originally called Customer Accounting System (CAS). Named after Creedence Clearwater Revival album 'Cosmos Factory'. Enabled a team of four to build an entire bank checking account system in one year.
Operating system later architected by Robert A. Siegel after he left PHI.
Two-digit year field vulnerability; Kastner fixed an early instance in Marine Midland Bank's bond portfolio system in July 1969, 30 years before the global Y2K crisis.
Command syntax for IBM OS to control program execution: tape mounts, disk pack allocation, memory reservation. Notoriously cryptic; a single misplaced comma could fail a job.
Primary input mechanism at PHI; programmers wrote code on coding forms, had it keypunched, submitted deck to operations queue, waited hours for printout.
Primary memory technology of the IBM 360 era; core dumps captured entire contents of core memory upon program failure.
Debugging technique: on catastrophic failure, OS printed entire core memory contents as hex. Could span hundreds of pages. Kastner learned to read these to find program errors.
Dense fold-out cheat sheet roughly placemat-sized; primary documentation resource for programmers at PHI in absence of online docs.
Business model where companies purchased CPU time on shared mainframes; PHI charged $360/hour for IBM 360/65 time in 1969.
Interactive terminal-based computing available at universities and research labs in 1969 but not typical at commercial service bureaus like PHI.
Banking application upgraded by Kastner in July 1969; contained two-digit year field vulnerability for bonds maturing in year 2000+.
Accounting system designed by Kastner for Cumberland Farms; required handling mechanical pump counter rollover at 9,999 gallons.
Technique exemplified by Cosmos: templates and rules produce architecturally consistent code at scale, sidestepping Brooks's Law coordination overhead.
Navigation code for the Apollo lunar program; PHI founders Philip Hankins and David Moros had written this code before founding PHI.
